setting.note: In the cocoa-boom port of Ilhéus on the Bahia coast around 1925, two stories run together: the Syrian bar-owner Nacib's love for his cinnamon-skinned cook Gabriela, and the town's struggle between the old cocoa colonels and the modernisers who want to open the harbour bar to ocean ships. Amado maps his own city: the bars and cathedral square of Ilhéus, the treacherous sandbar at the harbour mouth, and the cocoa plantations inland whose blood-soaked wealth built the whole coast.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Jorge Amado, first published 1958 (Gabriela, Cravo e Canela). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Gabriela, Clove and Cinnamon, Jorge Amado (1958). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The warm cinnamon of Gabriela's skin and of the spice and cocoa the whole coast lives by. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
